# SRC064 - Consumer Reactions to Drip Pricing

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Pricing & Offers |
| Authors / source | Morwitz et al. |
| Year | 2020 |
| Source type | Academic review |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/Morwitz-Vicki-Wroe-Alderson-PAPER-2020.pdf](https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/Morwitz-Vicki-Wroe-Alderson-PAPER-2020.pdf) |

## Bias / limitation

Academic; broad consumer pricing.

## Core takeaway

Partitioned pricing changes perception and can mislead if not transparent.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to keep SaaS pricing pages clear and reduce distrust.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.
